stemHtml: What is correct about an Air Data Computer (ADC)?

options.a: An ADC is an auxiliary system that provides altitude information in the event that the static source is blocked.
options.b: An ADC transforms air data measurements into electric impulses driving servo motors in instruments.
options.c: An ADC converts air data measurements given by ATC from the ground in order to provide correct altitude and speed information.
options.d: An ADC measures position error in the static system and transmits this information to ATC to provide correct altitude reporting.
